name.text: Calculating the total capacity of a rainwater harvesting tank

questiontext.text:
A school is installing a new rainwater harvesting tank. The design consists of a cylindrical main body with a conical roof to allow debris to slide off. The dimensions of the tank are:

  • Radius of the tank = {@r@} m
  • Height of the cylindrical part = {@h_cyl@} m
  • Vertical height of the conical roof = {@h_cone@} m
[[jsxgraph width="400px" height="450px"]] var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, { /* Bounding box accommodates the max possible heights (up to 20m total) */ boundingbox: [-12, 25, 12, -6], axis: false, showCopyright: false }); var R = {#r#}; var H_cyl = {#h_cyl#}; var H_cone = {#h_cone#}; var p = 0.3; /* Perspective squash factor */ /* 1. Base of Cylinder */ /* Front solid curve */ board.create('curve', [function(t){return R*Math.cos(t)}, function(t){return (R*p)*Math.sin(t)}, Math.PI, 2*Math.PI], {strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 2}); /* Back dashed curve */ board.create('curve', [function(t){return R*Math.cos(t)}, function(t){return (R*p)*Math.sin(t)}, 0, Math.PI], {strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 1, dash: 2}); /* 2. Seam (Where cylinder meets cone) */ board.create('curve', [function(t){return R*Math.cos(t)}, function(t){return H_cyl + (R*p)*Math.sin(t)}, Math.PI, 2*Math.PI], {strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 2}); board.create('curve', [function(t){return R*Math.cos(t)}, function(t){return H_cyl + (R*p)*Math.sin(t)}, 0, Math.PI], {strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 1, dash: 2}); /* 3. Vertical Sides of Cylinder */ board.create('segment', [[-R, 0], [-R, H_cyl]], {strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 2}); board.create('segment', [[R, 0], [R, H_cyl]], {strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 2}); /* 4. Conical Roof */ var apex = [0, H_cyl + H_cone]; board.create('segment', [[-R, H_cyl], apex], {strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 2}); board.create('segment', [[R, H_cyl], apex], {strokeColor: 'black', strokeWidth: 2}); /* Internal vertical dash for cone height */ board.create('segment', [[0, H_cyl], apex], {strokeColor: 'gray', strokeWidth: 1, dash: 2}); /* 5. Labels */ // Radius (Blue) board.create('segment', [[0, 0], [R, 0]], {strokeColor: 'blue', strokeWidth: 2}); board.create('text', [R/2, -1.8, "{#r#} m"], {color: 'blue', fontSize: 14, fontWeight: 'bold'}); // Cylinder Height (Red) board.create('segment', [[-R - 1.5, 0], [-R - 1.5, H_cyl]], {strokeColor: 'red', strokeWidth: 2}); board.create('text', [-R - 4.5, H_cyl/2, "{#h_cyl#} m"], {color: 'red', fontSize: 14, fontWeight: 'bold'}); // Cone Height (Green) board.create('segment', [[-R - 1.5, H_cyl], [-R - 1.5, H_cyl + H_cone]], {strokeColor: 'green', strokeWidth: 2}); board.create('text', [-R - 4.5, H_cyl + H_cone/2, "{#h_cone#} m"], {color: 'green', fontSize: 14, fontWeight: 'bold'}); [[/jsxgraph]]

Calculate the total capacity of the tank in litres. (Take $\pi = 3.142$)

Total Capacity = [[input:ans1]] litres [[validation:ans1]]

generalfeedback.text:
Worked Solution

To find the total capacity, we calculate the volume of the cylindrical body and the conical roof, then convert the total to litres.

Step 1: Volume of the Cylindrical Part (\(V_1\))

\begin{align} V_1 &= \pi r^2 h \\ &= \pi \times {@r@}^2 \times {@h_cyl@} \\ &= \pi \times {@r2@} \times {@h_cyl@} \\ &= {@V_cyl@} \text{ m}^3 \end{align}

Step 2: Volume of the Conical Roof (\(V_2\))

\begin{align} V_2 &= \frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h \\ &= \frac{1}{3} \times \pi \times {@r@}^2 \times {@h_cone@} \\ &= \frac{1}{3} \times \pi \times {@r2@} \times {@h_cone@} \\ &= {@V_cone@} \text{ m}^3 \end{align}

Step 3: Total Capacity in Litres

\begin{align} \text{Total Volume} &= V_1 + V_2 \\ &= {@V_cyl@} + {@V_cone@} \\ &= {@total_m3@} \text{ m}^3 \end{align}

Since \(1 \text{ m}^3 = 1,000 \text{ litres}\):

\begin{align} \text{Total Capacity} &= {@total_m3@} \times 1000 \\ &= \mathbf{{@ta@} \text{ litres}} \end{align}

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.

truefeedback.text: You have correctly calculated the volume of the tank but you failed to convert the units from m\(^3\) to litres. Please check the worked solution below.
